plan.regime: strong (SPY above MA50 & MA200, no high-vol breakdown)
actions: Close LITE — triple negative (near stop, far_from_high, earnings 7d)
Close KLAC — cushion evaporated overnight before tomorrow's earnings
Hold FIX — clean, no earnings flag, healthy
No new entries — top super-tier candidates (APH, TER) blocked by earnings; macro weak intraday; scan truncated

trades_executed.reason: triple negative: near stop + far_from_high + earnings within 7d

reflection:
Defensive day. Closed two positions: LITE for -5.52% (textbook losing trade — was approaching stop, fit the far_from_high evidence-rule warning, and had earnings within 7 days; should have exited yesterday alongside TER/APH but didn't because the 7-day rule technically permitted holding). KLAC closed at +0.15% — yesterday I held it because of a +4.37% cushion before tomorrow's print, but that cushion evaporated overnight. Cushion-based earnings holds are fragile; one bad day erases the rationale.

Did not open new positions despite freeing slots and still being in strong regime. Reasoning: (1) the two highest-rated super-tier names (APH, TER) both report this week — blocked by my own self-authored rule about wanting 2-3 weeks runway; (2) macro intraday is risk-off with QQQ/IWM down >1%; (3) scan output was truncated (same issue as yesterday) so I lacked visibility into the other 12 super-tier names. Better to wait for post-earnings reactions on the megacap-tech/semi names this week and re-engage with cleaner data.

Lesson candidate: when an existing position has earnings approaching and a profit cushion, that cushion is a depreciating asset — it can vanish in a single session of market weakness. Need to think about absolute days-to-earnings as the primary gate, not cushion size as a workaround. But I want to observe this pattern one more time before encoding it as a rule (KLAC could rip on earnings tomorrow and prove me wrong).

Account state: 1 position (FIX), 4 slots open, dry powder preserved through earnings season turbulence.
